creator: andrews@TECHNOLOGIST.COM
text:
On Sat, Apr 22, 2000 at 03:12:37AM +0900, nishida@csl.sony.co.jp wrote:
> >Synopsis:       NewPort: net/tcpillust

This does not work:

<2 5091-0> (00-04-22 15:49:42) [will@shadow /usr/ports/net/tcpillust]% make
===>  Configuring for tcpillust-1.0a
loading cache ./config.cache
checking for pcap library... 
checking for pcap_dispatch in -lpcap... yes
checking for tcl.h... /usr/local/include/tcl8.0/tcl.h
checking for tk.h... /usr/local/include/tk8.2/tk.h
checking for tcllib... checking for Tcl_Init in -ltcl... no

[...]

checking for tklib... checking for Tk_Init in -ltk... no
checking for Tk_Init in -ltk42... no
checking for Tk_Init in -ltk80... no
checking for Tk_Init in -ltk82... no
checking for Tk_Init in -ltk80jp... no
Fatal: can not find tklib, use --with-tk-lib option

Apparently its configure script is worthless and doesn't actually take into
account --prefix, --with-[tcl,tk]-lib, or --with-[tcl,tk]-include.

Note: I have TCL 8.0, 8.2, and Tk 8.2 installed. The configure script
apparently doesn't know how to handle this sort of situation.

-- 
Will Andrews <andrews@technologist.com>
